name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — implement per-conversation cost circuit-breaker
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — "What's Left" deferred item #9 (per-conv cost circuit-breaker); cross-ref `docs/plans/chat-agent-v1.md` §5.6 + §17.v1.1.

## Problem / gap
v1 only carries a *minimal* per-conv cost cap. The full circuit-breaker — soft warnings at 80%, separate caps for read-only vs write tools, integration with daily/monthly budgets — is unimplemented. The audit flags this as a ship-gate risk for the chat agent at 0.7.0: a runaway tool loop or model can burn through arbitrary cost without intervention.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-conv cost ledger tracks tokens + tool-call cost across the loop's lifetime
- [ ] Soft warning emitted at 80% of cap (panel-visible + tracing event)
- [ ] Separate caps configurable for read-only vs write tool effects
- [ ] Integration with daily / monthly budget rollups in `Config` (or documented stub)
- [ ] Hard halt when cap is exceeded; surface as typed `agent.chat` error
- [ ] Unit tests for breach paths (warn, halt, daily-rollover)
- [ ] Documented in `docs/plans/agent-core-v1.md` follow-ups section

## Dependencies
- Blocks: 0.7.0 release-gate sign-off (audit-flagged ship risk)
- Blocked-by: none (current cap stub is in tree)

## Notes
Current cap location: search for `cost` / `budget` in `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/loop_core.rs` and `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/service.rs`. The audit flagged this as the single deferred-item that *might* warrant 0.7.x ship-gate treatment vs. the rest of the 0.8.x v1.1 backlog.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — thread per-iteration CancellationToken into run_tool_loop
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — Critical path #1; `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/service.rs:209` `TODO(Phase D2)`; deferred item #7.

## Problem / gap
AC-10 cancel only catches whole-turn cancellations via outer `tokio::select!`. Per-iteration cancellation inside `loop_core::run_tool_loop` requires a `&CancellationToken` parameter threaded through `AgentLoop::handle_turn` and observed at the top of each iteration. Today an in-flight tool call cannot be interrupted mid-turn; the user must wait for the current iteration to drain.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `AgentLoop::handle_turn` accepts `cancel: &CancellationToken`
- [ ] `loop_core::run_tool_loop` checks the token at iteration boundary
- [ ] `AgentService::dispatch` threads the per-conv token through
- [ ] Existing `agent.chat.cancel` RPC test extended to assert mid-turn break
- [ ] No regression on the 12 existing `#[tokio::test]` blocks in `loop_core.rs`

## Dependencies
- Blocks: smoother long-tool-call UX in panel
- Blocked-by: none

## Notes
Estimated ~150 LoC, single commit. Files: `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/loop_core.rs`, `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/service.rs`.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — expose public chain.append RPC for soul promote + agent journal

The trait shape is forward-compatible; switching when the wire ships is a drop-in.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New daemon RPC arm accepting a `WitnessRecord` payload
- [ ] New types in `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/protocol.rs`
- [ ] `weaver soul promote` switched from local-audit-log fallback to RPC call
- [ ] Local-audit-log fallback retained as offline mode (or removed with rollout doc)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the new RPC arm
- [ ] `TODO(agent-core-v1.1)` removed

~250 LoC + tests. Files: `crates/clawft-weave/src/daemon.rs`, `crates/clawft-weave/src/commands/soul_cmd.rs:239-265`, `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/protocol.rs`.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — promote workspace/global PermissionResolver split
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — Critical path #3; `crates/clawft-core/src/bootstrap.rs:633` `TODO(v1.1)`.

## Problem / gap
Workspace overlay is deep-merged into `config.routing` upstream in `config_loader::load_config_raw`, so workspace policy reaches the resolver but the security-ceiling pattern (`enforce_workspace_ceiling` clamping workspace permissions against system-wide bounds) is bypassed. Fine for single-user kernels; required for multi-tenant.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `PermissionResolver::new(global, Some(workspace))` two-arg signature
- [ ] `enforce_workspace_ceiling` clamps workspace permissions against global ceiling
- [ ] Loader splits workspace from global at the loader layer (not via deep merge)
- [ ] Tests for `workspace.write > global.write` clamps to global
- [ ] Tests for `workspace.read < global.read` keeps workspace
- [ ] `TODO(v1.1)` removed

## Dependencies
- Blocks: any multi-tenant deployment
- Blocked-by: none

## Notes
~300 LoC + tests. Files: `crates/clawft-core/src/bootstrap.rs`, `crates/clawft-platform/src/config_loader.rs`, `crates/clawft-core/src/pipeline/permissions.rs`.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — stabilize append_turns_are_monotonic flake via injectable clock
description_md:

- [ ] `SubstrateConversationSink` accepts an injectable clock (e.g. `dyn Clock` or `Arc<dyn Fn() -> SystemTime>`)
- [ ] Test uses a deterministic clock yielding same-ms appends
- [ ] Production path keeps `SystemTime::now()` default
- [ ] Test flake re-run 100× shows zero failures

- Blocks: confidence in CI rotation
- Blocked-by: none

## Notes
File: `crates/clawft-service-agent/src/substrate_sink.rs`.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — plumb tool_calls / token / model fields through OutboundMessage
description_md:
## Source

`AgentChatResult` fields `tool_calls`, `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `model`, `identity_source` default to empty/zero/None at the wire because `OutboundMessage` is a generic bus envelope without these fields. Loop's result type needs enriching to thread them through; tests in `protocol.rs:179-201` pin the C1 shape so the panel keeps tolerating defaults across the cutover.

- [ ] Loop populates them on every turn (zeros become real values)

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — notify-driven hot-reload watcher for identity files
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — deferred item #16; `crates/clawft-core/src/agent/identity.rs:36-38`.

## Problem / gap
`FileIdentityProvider` re-reads `.clawft/SOUL.md` + `IDENTITY.md` on every call. Files are small so it's cheap today, but a `notify`-driven watcher with cache invalidation "arrives when measurement says it earns its keep."

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Watcher gated behind a feature flag (or always-on if cheap)
- [ ] Invalidates cache on file change
- [ ] Re-reads on next call
- [ ] Test verifies edit-then-call returns new contents
- [ ] No regression on platforms without notify

## Dependencies
- Blocked-by: none (low-priority — measurement-driven)

## Notes
Measurement first. Profile current re-read cost before landing watcher.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — observability path logging router decisions to substrate
description_md:

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — per-tool Permit token + proof-of-permission API
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — In-source v1.1 follow-up at `loop_core.rs:1070-1073`; `kernel_gate.rs:24, 66, 89`.

## Problem / gap
Per-tool `Permit { token }` is currently discarded — the plan calls out "optionally pass the token to tools.execute" as a follow-up that requires a tool-side proof-of-permission API the registry doesn't yet expose. Kernel-side `Deny { reason, receipt }` also drops `receipt` because the panel UX has nowhere to render it. `Permit::token: Option<Vec<u8>>` is hex-encoded but only "kernel-permit" sentinel propagates when None.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `ToolRegistry::execute` accepts an optional `Permit` token
- [ ] Tools verify-or-ignore the token (back-compat)
- [ ] Kernel `Deny.receipt` plumbed to panel
- [ ] `Permit.token` propagates real token bytes, not sentinel
- [ ] Tests for token round-trip + receipt display

## Dependencies
- Blocked-by: panel UX work (cross-stream W-UI/15)

## Notes
Proof-of-permission is the security upgrade; current state is advisory.

name: ws11: agent-core-v1.1 — hard-refuse on binding-thread mismatch (governance rule)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/11-agent-core-v1.md` — In-source v1.1 follow-up at `identity.rs:57`; deferred item #13.

## Problem / gap
Binding-thread mismatch today annotates the prompt and emits a `warn!` — does not refuse to run. Hard refusal is v1.1. Today's check is at load time only (SHA-256 hash + `BINDING_THREAD_EXCERPT` substring match in `IdentityLoader`). v1.1 promotes this to a governance rule (`soul.binding_thread_intact`) evaluated by `gate.check` on every turn.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New governance rule `soul.binding_thread_intact`
- [ ] Evaluated by `gate.check` on every turn (not just load time)
- [ ] Hard refusal path: `Deny { reason: "binding-thread mismatch" }`
- [ ] Configurable: warn-only (current) vs deny (v1.1 default)
- [ ] Tests for both modes

## Dependencies
- Blocked-by: none

## Notes
Security-relevant: prevents prompt-injection from disabling SOUL binding.

name: ws16: browser — wasm-bindgen-test regression suite for init() + send_message() pipeline
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16-browser-wasm.md` — P0.4; `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16b-browser-pipeline-wire-plan.md` — "What did NOT land".

## Problem / gap
The full 6-stage pipeline now runs on every browser turn (16b wire-through landed in this session). However NO `wasm-bindgen-test` harness exists in the workspace — `grep wasm_bindgen_test crates/` returns zero. Without a regression suite, the freshly-wired pipeline can silently break on the next refactor. The audit flags this as a 0.7.x ship-gate concern: "no test coverage = regression risk."

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crates/clawft-wasm/tests/browser_*.rs` covers: `BrowserHttpClient` round-trip, `BrowserFileSystem` write/read/list/delete, `init()` + `send_message()` happy-path against a local fake LLM endpoint
- [ ] `wasm-pack test --headless` runs in CI via `wasm-browser.yml`
- [ ] At least one regression test asserts the 6-stage pipeline path is reached (not the bypass) — e.g. by inspecting a side-effect from the classifier or scorer stage
- [ ] Test runtime fits the existing CI budget

## Dependencies
- Blocks: 0.7.0 release-gate sign-off (audit-flagged regression risk)
- Blocked-by: none — pipeline wire-through is in tree

## Notes
The 16b plan explicitly defers this as P0.4. Without it any future refactor of `pipeline/browser_llm_adapter.rs` or the `?Send` `async_trait` can silently revert to LLM-direct without anyone noticing.

name: ws16: browser — binary size budget audit (1.32 MB → wasm-opt -Oz CI gate)
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16-browser-wasm.md` — P0.5, P1.5; `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16b-browser-pipeline-wire-plan.md` — "What did NOT land".

## Problem / gap
Wiring the full pipeline grew the bindgen artifact from ~840 KB to ~1.32 MB — well over the planning target of <300 KB raw / <120 KB gzip. `wasm-opt -Oz` is not yet wired into `wasm-browser.yml`. No CI size gate. The `wasm-browser-size` job referenced in P1.5 was never added. The audit flags this as a 0.7.x ship-gate concern: a 1.32 MB unoptimized bundle without a budget check is a real risk for browser users.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `wasm-opt -Oz` wired into `wasm-browser.yml`
- [ ] Step summary captures raw + gzip sizes after `wasm-opt`
- [ ] CI fails PRs that bust the documented budget (or relax the budget in writing with a documented rationale)
- [ ] Decision documented: keep <300 KB target or relax to <X KB
- [ ] `pr-gates.yml` has the `wasm-browser-size` job (P1.5 follow-through)
- [ ] First post-`wasm-opt` raw + gzip numbers checked in to `docs/browser/`

## Dependencies
- Blocks: 0.7.0 release-gate sign-off
- Blocked-by: none

## Notes
Accept that pipeline wire-through cost real bytes — but at least gate further drift.

name: ws16: browser — streaming chat via ReadableStream / wasm-streams
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16b-browser-pipeline-wire-plan.md` — "What did NOT land"; `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16-browser-wasm.md` — "Streaming via ReadableStream".

## Problem / gap
`StreamCallback = Box<dyn FnMut(&str) -> bool + Send>` is incompatible with the browser's single-threaded `!Send` model. `PipelineRegistry::complete_stream` and `LlmTransport::complete_stream` default impl are gated `#[cfg(not(feature = "browser"))]`. A browser streaming entry needs a `?Send` callback type or migration to `wasm-streams` / `ReadableStream` SSE parser. `browser_delay()` is also a no-op `yield`; should upgrade to `gloo-timers`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Browser-flavoured stream callback type without `Send` bound (or `wasm-streams` rewrite)
- [ ] Browser streaming path through the pipeline
- [ ] `browser_delay()` migrated to `gloo-timers` so backoff actually waits
- [ ] CORS-proxy compatibility for SSE
- [ ] Test exercising streamed chunks via the pipeline

## Dependencies
- Blocked-by: design choice (callback vs `wasm-streams`)

## Notes
0.8.x. Pairs naturally with the W-UI/15 streaming UI work and with WS11 voice + streaming Phase 2.

name: ws16: browser — wire set_env to BrowserEnvironment via OnceLock<BrowserRuntime>
description_md:
## Source
`.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16-browser-wasm.md` — P0.3; `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/16b-browser-pipeline-wire-plan.md` — "What did NOT land"; `crates/clawft-wasm/src/lib.rs:353-355`; step6-bw6 "set_env wiring".

## Problem / gap
After pipeline wire-through, `BrowserPlatform` is moved into `AgentLoop`, so `BrowserRuntime` no longer retains a handle to the live `BrowserEnvironment`. `set_env` is documented as a no-op with a TODO. JS callers cannot mutate live env state. `BrowserPlatform::with_env` is the only constructor that takes pre-populated env, and `init()` doesn't expose a way to seed it from JS.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `BrowserRuntime` stores `Arc<BrowserEnvironment>` clone alongside the agent
- [ ] `set_env(key, value)` mutates through it
- [ ] `init()` accepts a pre-seeded env map (optional)
- [ ] Test: set_env then read via Platform.env() returns the new value
- [ ] Doc updated

## Dependencies
- Blocked-by: none — wire-through landed; just needs the handle

## Notes
Historic BW5/BW6 follow-up; now blocked only by 16b's design (move-into-AgentLoop).
